etymology.description: Depicts a newborn baby upside down; inverted 子(子) (zǐ, "child") with three lines representing fluid or hair
etymology.interpretation: The character 㐬 illustrates a newborn child positioned upside down, with an inverted 子(子) (zǐ, "child") symbolizing the baby, while the three lines below suggest the amniotic fluid surrounding it or hair. This visual representation captures the essence of birth and the newborn state.
